IMAGE 1:
URL: https://globalhealthdata.org/wp-content/uploads/2019/12/GHDMPP-Figure-18.1.jpg
DESCRIPTION: A circular diagram illustrating the steps in epidemiological investigation, including identifying disease burden, determining causes, testing interventions, synthesizing evidence, evaluating programs, and contributing to health policy planning, with a highlighted step on health policy and program planning.

IMAGE 2:
URL: http://intellspot.com/wp-content/uploads/2017/09/10-Key-Types-of-Data-Analysis-Methods-Infographic.png
DESCRIPTION: The content outlines ten key types of data analysis methods, including descriptive analysis, regression analysis, factor analysis, dispersion analysis, discriminant analysis, time series analysis, neural networks, decision trees, evolutionary algorithms, and fuzzy logic, each accompanied by concise explanations of their functions and applications.

DESCRIPTION: None

URL: https://ourworldindata.org/uploads/2016/08/Trends-in-global-healthcare-tracers-1-713x550.jpg
DESCRIPTION: A line graph displaying trends in global health indicators from 2000 to 2015, highlighting improvements in areas such as immunization, improved sanitation, antenatal care visits, and ART for people living with HIV.

IMAGE 5:
URL: https://www.psmarketresearch.com/img/GLOBAL-BIG-DATA-ANALYTICS-IN-HEALTHCARE-MARKET.png
DESCRIPTION: A graphic illustrates global big data analytics in the healthcare market, highlighting North America as the largest market in 2017, the Asia-Pacific region as the fastest-growing market, and presenting market size figures for 2017 and 2023 along with a growth rate of 21.8% from 2018 to 2023.

enriched_points.expanded_content: Statistical methods utilize diverse techniques, including descriptive and analytical approaches, to derive insights from health data, facilitating informed decision-making in global health initiatives.
Machine learning algorithms enhance data analysis by identifying complex patterns and relationships within large datasets, improving the accuracy of health predictions and interventions.

Predictive modeling employs statistical techniques to forecast health outcomes based on historical data, enabling proactive healthcare strategies and resource allocation for better public health management.
